statement: It is a vaccination certificate that should be able to be requested by organizers of events upon entry.
It is more money on defense that should be spent by the Netherlands.
It is all parents that should have free childcare for at least three days a week.
It is the European Union (EU) that should be exited by the Netherlands.
It is a tax per kilometer driven for motorists that should be introduced instead of the tax on car ownership.
It is decorative fireworks that should be allowed to be set off again during the upcoming New Year's Eve.
It is an additional tax that should be imposed on purchasing meat.
It is public broadcasting that should be given less funding.
It is a national healthcare fund for everyone that should be established instead of the existing health insurance companies.
It is the ban on face-covering clothing that should be abolished by the government.
It is where new residential areas are built that should be decided by the national government, rather than provinces and municipalities.
It is the VAT on cultural activities that should be reduced by the government to 5 percent.
It is a new nuclear power plant that should be built by the Netherlands.
It is the same tax benefits as households with two working partners that should be received by households with two partners, one of whom works.
It is the historical slave trade that should be apologized for by the Dutch government.
It is the opportunity to block laws passed by parliament through a referendum that should be given to citizens.
It is as much as secondary school teachers that should be earned by primary school teachers.
It is an additional flight tax for short-distance flights that should be introduced by the Netherlands.
It is integration that should be completed by asylum seekers with a temporary residence permit before getting a rental home.
It is both the purchase and sale of soft drugs by coffee shops that should be legalized by the government.
It is Dutch-language education that should be made more frequently mandatory at universities and colleges by the government.
It is assistance with suicide that should be able to be received by people who consider their lives complete.
It is an increase in welfare benefits that should no longer automatically be the result of an increase in minimum wages.
It is no new restrictions on the activities of farming businesses that should be imposed.
It is a middle school that relative authorities should establish so that students make a choice between vocational education, general secondary education, or pre-university education at a later age.
It is more refugees than it currently does that should be accepted by the Netherlands.
It is measures that should be taken by the state to redistribute wealth from the rich to the poor.
It is spending on public health care that must be increased by the government, even if this means increasing taxes.
It is the working day that should be reduced without reducing workers' wages.
It is the economy that should be intervened by governments as little as possible.
It is the Green Transition that the government should raise taxes on fossil fuels to finance.
It is irrigated agricultural areas that should be increased by the future Spanish government by means of large water transfers.
It is more sovereignty that must be recovered by us to better defend Spain's interests in Europe.
It is the strengthening of NATO in Europe that should be promoted by the Spanish government.
It is for its citizens' future to be able to be voted on by themselves in a referendum that the best way to solve the conflict in Catalonia is.
It is the State that is weakened by negotiating with pro-independence supporters.
It is Spain's territorial decentralization that must be deepened by relative authorities.
It is the right to self-determination that the Constitution must recognize.
It is Spanish that is endangered by the policies of linguistic immersion in the native language of bilingual Autonomous Communities.
It is the Law of Democratic Memory passed during this legislature that be repealed is necessary.
It is immigrants' own health services that should be paid by themselves.
It is access for all people that housing prices must be regulated by relative authorities to ensure.
It is all highways that are to have a general speed limit applied to them.
It is German defense spending that is to be increased by themselves.
It is Bundestag elections that the government should allow young people over the age of 16 to vote in.
It is subsidies for wind energy that the government is to end.
It is the ability of landlords to increase housing rents that the law is to limit more strictly.
It is covid-19 vaccines that patents are to continue to protect.
It is the phase-out of coal-fired power generation planned for 2038 that the government is to bring forward.
It is the statutory pension scheme that the government is to require all employed persons to be insured in.
It is the right of recognized refugees to join their families that relative authorities are to abolish.
It is revenue generated in Germany from digital services that the government is to levy a national tax on.
It is the traditional family of father, mother and children that should be promoted by the government more strongly than other living arrangements.
It is donations from companies to political parties that relative authorities should continue to permit donations from companies to political parties.
It is BAföG that should be received by students regardless of their parents' income.
It is different gender identities that are to be taken into linguistic account by federal authorities in their publications.
It is the Nord Stream 2 Baltic Sea pipeline, which transports gas from Russia to Germany that the government is to allow to go into operation as planned.
It is the solidarity surcharge that the government is to abolish completely.
It is headscarves that are to be allowed to be worn by female civil servants while on duty.
It is more financial support for projects to combat anti-Semitism that is to be provided by the federal government.
It is contracts for the expansion of the communications infrastructure in Germany that should not be allowed to be received by Chinese companies.
It is church tax that should continue to be collected by the state for religious communities.
It is the controlled sale of cannabis that is to be generally permitted by the government.
It is the European Union that is to be left by Germany.
It is the state lists of the parties for the elections to the German Bundestag that the parties are to fill alternately by women and men.
It is inpatient treatment in hospitals that hospitals are to continue to charge on the basis of a flat rate per case.
It is high assets that the government is to levy a tax on again.
It is video surveillance in public places that the government should allow facial recognition software to be used for.
It is tax breaks that should continue to be received by married couples without children.
It is organic agriculture that the government should promote more strongly than conventional agriculture.
It is Islamic associations that the state is to be able to recognize as religious communities.
It is the debt brake in the Basic Law that the parliament is to retain.
It is politically persecuted persons that the government is to continue to grant asylum only to.
It is the statutory minimum wage that the government is to increase to at least 12 euros by 2022 at the latest.
It is air traffic that the government is to tax more heavily.
It is for themselves that whether to allow their employees to work from home is to be decided by companies.
It is Polish domestic policy that should be less influenced by the European Union.
It is the effects of inflation on citizens that the government should increase social transfers to reduce.
It is private visits to specialists that should be financed by the state if the waiting time at a public facility exceeds three months.
It is a free nursery place that should be provided by the state for every child.
It is the content covered in the curriculum that more freedom should be given to schools to choose.
It is low-rent apartments that should be built by the state for rent.
It is top earners that the government should increase taxes for.
It is early retirement that the government should introduce for those who have worked a certain number of years, regardless of their age.
It is abortion laws that the parliament should liberalize.
It is the same health premium that should be paid by all entrepreneurs regardless of income.
It is the migrant relocation solutions adopted by the European Union that should be adopted by Poland.
It is the share of defense spending in Poland's GDP that the government should further increase.
It is grain imports from Ukraine that should be blocked by Poland.
It is public media funding from the state budget that the government should limit.
It is the powers of the secret services to track the activities of citizens on the Internet that relative authorities should limit.
It is Hungary's sovereignty that is threatened by the EU's rule of law mechanism.
It is Hungarian economic interests that should guide Hungarian foreign policy solely.
It is by referendum that whether to remain part of the EU should be decided by Hungary.
It is environmental influences (e.g. media content, sensitising activities) that can influence gender identity.
It is stronger state regulation of the work of NGOs supported by foreign organisations that the state need to implement.
It is the European Public Prosecutor's Office that should be joined by Hungary.
It is more effective law enforcement that needs the disclosure of the origin of criminals.
It is 18 that the government should raise the age of compulsory schooling back to.
It is at least the OECD average of 5.2 per cent (GDP) that the government should increase education spending to.
It is teachers' salaries that should be doubled by relative authorities.
It is political influence that changing the university model (reorganisation into a trust) has reduced.
It is only men and women that the government should allow to marry.
It is a closer ratio of men to women that should be striven for by parties when lists are drawn up.
It is targeted measures that should be taken by the state to promote equal participation of fathers and mothers in child-rearing.
It is the Istanbul Convention which combats violence against women and domestic violence that should be ratified by the Hungarian government.
It is comprehensive public procurement reform that the government needs to implement (e.g. opening up large-scale centralised public procurement to smaller firms).
It is the labour market that people are helped by public employment to re-enter.
It is the current three-month unemployment benefit that the government should extend.
It is in Hungary that Hungary should legalise the use of medical cannabis.
It is an independent Ministry of Health that the government should establish.
It is the President of the Hungarian Republic that voters should directly elect.
It is comprehensive reform of the electoral system (redrawing of district boundaries, abolition of winner-take-all compensation, extension of postal voting) that the government needs to implement.
It is a legal framework for primary elections that the government should provide.
It is polluting companies that the government should tax more heavily.
It is car traffic that the government should limit through various measures (P+R parking, construction of cycle paths, improvement of public transport) in larger cities.
It is a broad social dialogue that is needed by the redevelopment of urban green spaces (e.g. the Liget project in Budapest).
It is an independent ministry for the environment that needs to be established by the government.
It is an animal rights commissioner that the government should introduce.
It is the Eurozone that should be left by Italy.
It is a common foreign policy that should be adopted by the European Union.
It is European economic integration that has gone too far: it is more autonomy that should be regained by member states.
It is Migrant landings that the government must stop, even by extreme means.
It is Italian citizenship (ius scholae) that Italy should grant to children who were born in Italy to foreign citizens and who have completed their schooling.
It is homosexual, bisexual, transgender (LGBT+) people that the government should grant more civil rights to.
It is freedom of choice in end-of-life matters (euthanasia) that the government should guarantee.
It is recreational use of marijuana/cannabis that should be allowed by the government.
It is companies that relative authorities need to pass a law to prevent their production from being relocated abroad.
It is a heritage tax one's wealth that relative authorities should introduce.
It is employees that should be able to be fired more easily by businesses.
It is health care that only the state, not private individuals, should manage.
It is the Italian economy that would be benefited by the introduction of a single income tax rate ("flat tax").
It is an hourly minimum wage that the government should introduce.
It is the use of nuclear power plants for the purpose of producing energy that the government should promote.
It is more incinerators/thermal power plants that should be built by Italy.
It is its foreign policy that should be kept aligned with the choices of the Atlantic Alliance (NATO) by Italy itself.
It is arms and war material that should no longer be sent to the Ukrainian government by Italy.
It is in the region itself that the region should fully retain taxes collected at the regional level.
It is separation of careers between judges and prosecutors that should be introduced by the government.
It is direct election of the President of the Republic that should be introduced by the government.
It is the retirement age that should be increased.
It is more funding that should be allocated by the federal government for health insurance premium subsidies.
It is to 150% of the maximum individual AHV pension (capping) that the government currently limits the pension for married couples. It is this limit that the government should eliminate.
It is pensions that the government is to reduce (lowering the minimum conversion rate from 6.8% to 6%) as part of the reform of the BVG (occupational pension plan).
It is today's 14 weeks of maternity leave and two weeks of paternity leave that relative authorities should increase paid parental leave beyond.
It is more financial support that should be provided by the federal government for public housing construction.
It is compulsory vaccination of children that the government should introduce based on the Swiss vaccination plan.
It is foods containing sugar that a tax (sugar tax) should be introduced on.
It is health care costs that more should be contributed to by insured persons (e.g., increase the minimum deductible).
It is children with learning difficulties or disabilities that teachers should teach in regular classes according to the Swiss integrated schooling concept.
It is the requirements for the high school that should be raised by the federal government.
It is the conditions for naturalization that should be relaxed by the government (e.g., by shortening the residence period).
It is more qualified workers from non-EU/EFTA countries that the authorities should allow to work in Switzerland (increase third-country quota).
It is efforts to house asylum seekers in centers outside Europe during the asylum procedure that should be made.
It is the right to vote and stand for election at the municipal level that the government should grant foreign nationals who have lived in Switzerland for at least ten years.
It is cannabis use that the government should legalize.
It is direct active euthanasia that relative authorities should allow doctors to administer.
It is the same rights as heterosexual couples in all areas that should be given to same-sex couples.
It is tax cuts that should be implemented at the federal level over the next four years.
It is married couples that the government tax separately (individual taxation).
It is on all inheritances over one million Swiss francs that a national inheritance tax should be introduced.
It is the differences between cantons with high and low financial capacity that should be further reduced by the authorities through fiscal equalization.
It is a minimum wage of CHF 4,000 for all full-time employees that the government should introduce.
It is stricter regulations that should be imposed on the financial sector (e.g., stricter capital requirements for banks, ban on bonuses).
It is their electricity supplier that should be free to be chosen by private households (complete liberalization of the electricity market).
It is housing construction regulations that the government should relax (e.g., noise protection, occupancy rates).
It is stricter controls on equal pay for women and men that should be imposed.
It is busy sections of highways that relative authorities should widen.
It is the registration of new passenger cars with internal combustion engines that should be banned by Switzerland starting in 2035.
It is incentives and target agreements that should rely exclusively on, rather than on bans and restrictions, to achieve climate targets.
It is the construction of new nuclear power plants that relative authorities should allow again.
It is a comprehensive public service offering also in rural regions that should be guaranteed by the state.
It is increasing electricity tariffs when consumption is higher (progressive electricity tariffs) that the government should introduce.
It is the protection regulations for large predators (lynx, wolf, bear) that should be relaxed by relative authorities.
It is farmers with proof of ecological performance that relative authorities should grant direct payments only to.
It is stricter animal welfare regulations that should be introduced for livestock (e.g. permanent access to outdoor areas).
It is preserving biodiversity that Swiss authorities should dedicate 30% of Switzerland's land area to.
It is a ban on single-use plastic and non-recyclable plastics that should be imposed.

It is government measures to make the use of electronic devices more sustainable (e.g., right to repair, extension of warranty period, minimum guaranteed period for software updates) that should be introduced.
It is additional powers in the area of digitization of government services that Relative authorities should give so that it can impose binding directives and standards on the cantons.
It is a stronger regulation that should be imposed on the major Internet platforms (i.e., transparency rules on algorithms, increased liability for content, combating disinformation).
It is a popular initiative aims to reduce television and radio fees (CHF 200 per household, exemption for businesses) that should be launched.
It is 16 that the government should lower the voting age to.
It is a referendum on federal spending above a certain amount (optional financial referendum) that should be possible to be held.
It is their cooperation with NATO that should be expanded by the Swiss Armed Forces.

It is other states that relative authorities should allow the Federal Council to authorize to re-export Swiss weapons in cases of a war of aggression in violation of international law (e.g., the attack on Ukraine).
It is automatic facial recognition that the government should ban in public spaces.
It is the Schengen agreement with the EU that should be terminated by Switzerland and it is more security checks that should be reintroduced directly on the border.
It is closer relations with the European Union (EU) that should be established.
It is a comprehensive free trade agreement (including agriculture) with the USA that should be strived for by Switzerland.
It is the Bilateral Agreements with the EU that should be terminated by Switzerland and it is a free trade agreement without the free movement of persons that should be sought.
It is a strict interpretation of neutrality that should be returned to by Switzerland (renounce economic sanctions to a large extent).
